title: Telehealth To Inperson Escalation Planning
description: A scheduled-care coordination workflow that aligns appointments, dependencies, and staff/resource availability across clinical units. Focus case: telehealth to inperson escalation planning.
assumptions: Cases are instantiated before resource reservations; actions assume explicit intake step (A1) precedes most flows.
Resource tokens (providers, rooms, equipment) represent exclusive reservations that must be consumed and returned by matching reserve/release actions.
Specialty taxonomy (T10) is distinct from service instances (T13) to avoid conflating preferred vs reserved specialty semantics.
risks: Ambiguity between 'specialty' and 'specialist_service' could lead to incorrect lifecycle handling; preserve separation in implementation.
Using a generic supertype for teams risks obscuring coordination vs escalation responsibilities; concrete team types must be enforced.
Resource contention (providers, equipment) may cause deadlock if reservation and release actions are not sequenced transactionally.
